title: Text Processing (sed / awk / friends)
html:
Text Processing (sed / awk / friends)

Contents

  • Cut / sort / uniq
  • tr / paste / column
  • sed (stream editor)
  • awk
  • xargs
  • jq (JSON)

Cut / sort / uniq

CommandDescription
 
cut -d: -f1 /etc/passwd
first colon-separated field
cut -c1-10 file
first 10 characters per line
sort file
alphabetical
sort -n file
numeric
sort -h file
human (1K, 2M…)
sort -r file
reverse
sort -k 3,3 file
by 3rd field
sort -u file
sort + dedupe
uniq -c sorted_file
count consecutive duplicates
sort file | uniq -c | sort -rn
frequency-rank lines

tr / paste / column

CommandDescription
 
tr A-Z a-z < file
uppercase → lowercase
tr -d '\r' < file
strip CR (DOS → Unix)
tr -s ' '
squeeze runs of spaces
paste a.txt b.txt
side-by-side merge
column -t file
align by whitespace

sed (stream editor)

CommandDescription
 
sed 's/foo/bar/g' file
global substitute
sed -i 's/foo/bar/g' file
in-place
sed -i.bak 's/x/y/' file
in-place with backup
sed -n '5,10p' file
print lines 5–10
sed '/pattern/d' file
delete lines matching pattern
sed -E 's/([0-9]+)/<\1>/g' file
extended regex with capture
sed -i '$ a final line' file
append a line at end

awk

CommandDescription
 
awk '{print $1}' file
first column (whitespace-split)
awk -F: '{print $1,$7}' /etc/passwd
field separator
awk '$3 > 1000' /etc/passwd
lines where 3rd field > 1000
awk '{sum+=$1} END{print sum}'
sum first column
awk '!seen[$0]++'
dedupe while preserving order
awk 'NR==FNR{a[$1]=1;next} $1 in a' a.txt b.txt
join: keep b lines where col1 in a

xargs

CommandDescription
 
find . -name '*.tmp' | xargs rm
feed paths to rm
ls *.txt | xargs -I{} mv {} dst/
with placeholder
xargs -P 8 -n 1 curl -s < urls.txt
8 parallel curls

jq (JSON)

CommandDescription
 
jq . file.json
pretty-print
jq -r '.users[].name' file.json
raw strings
jq 'select(.age > 30)' file.json
filter
curl -s api/users | jq '.[] | .id'
extract IDs
